characters.description: Agent RepairmanBiographical informationStatusUnknownAffiliationDepartment of EnergyOccupationAgentPhysical informationEye colorBluePortrayalMain actorRobert Walker BranchaudAppears in2,5,6 Anunnamed male, portrayed byRobert Walker Branchaud, was an unnamed minor character in thefirst seasonofStranger Things. He was an undercover agent forHawkins National Laboratory, posing as a repairman. After discovering thatWill Byershad been taken by acreaturefromanother dimension, Dr.Martin Brennerdecided to investigate theByers residence. An agent disguised as a repairman knocked on the Byers' front door to see if anyone was home. When he received no answer, he notified Brenner, as well as a few other agents, that the premises was clear for investigation.

Robert AkersBiographical informationStatusDeceasedDiedNovember 6,1987AliasesLieutenant AkersLt. AkersResidenceHawkins,IndianaRelationsDr. Kay(boss)Jack Sullivan(boss)Luis Ramirez†(ally)Eleven(target/killer)Jim Hopper(enemy)Kali Prasad†(enemy/victim)AffiliationUnited States Military Police CorpsWolf PackOccupationSoldierU.S Army LieutenantPhysical informationHeight6’4"Eye colorBlueHair colorBrownPortrayalMain actorAlex BreauxAppears in35,36,37,38,39,41,42 Lt. Akers Akers is initially presented as impatient and force-driven, frequently using dehumanizing language toward Subject 011 (“Eleven”) and framing her as a dangerous anomaly rather than a child. His speech and conduct reflect a strict threat-based worldview, prioritizing containment over empathy. As the situation escalates—particularly following the destruction of military assets and personnel—Akers’ behavior shifts from procedural enforcement to heightened urgency and independent action. His interactions with Dr. Kay reveal growing frustration with what he perceives as ineffective leadership and repetitive strategic failure. Rather than blind obedience, Akers demonstrates increasing situational awareness regarding the scale of the threat emerging from the gate. His remarks and decisions suggest that, over time, he recognizes the broader danger posed by the Upside Down itself, even as his methods remain aggressive and uncompromising.

characters.name: Stacey Albright
characters.description: Stacey Albright198919851984Biographical informationStatusAliveBorn1971Age13(season two)14(season three)17-18(epilogue)ResidenceHawkins,IndianaRelationship statusSingleOther relationsDustin Henderson(former enemy, possible crush)[1]Eleven(enemy)Max Mayfield(enemy)OccupationStudent atHawkins Middle School(1982-1985)Student atHawkins High School(1985-1989)Physical informationEye colorBrownHair colorAuburnPortrayalMain actorSydney BullockAppears in17,19,42Stacey Albright,[2]portrayed bySydney Bullock, is a recurring character in thesecond,thirdandfifthseasons ofStranger Things. She is a snobby student in the same grade asthe Party. Born in 1971, Stacey Albright was presumably raised inHawkins,Indiana. Stacey began attendingHawkins Middle Schoolin the fall of 1982. DuringHawkins Middle School’sannualSnow Ball, Stacey attended the dance along with her friends. A snazzyDustin Hendersonapproached her and asked her to dance, but Stacey chuckled and sarcastically declined before walking away. However, Stacey is shocked upon high-schoolerNancy Wheelerdancing with Dustin. Stacey appears to be arrogant and conceited, traits that are likely a result of, and fortified by, her popularity. A primary example would be when she rudely rejected Dustin for asking her to dance with him, and was later shocked to see him dancing withMike Wheeler's older sister,Nancy. Months later, her behavior seems to have not changed, as displayed by how she appeared amused by El and Max trying to walk in high heels. By the time she graduated high school, she appears to be less arrogant, as she respects Dustin for rebelling during his valedictorian speech, and invites him and his friends to her graduation party.

characters.name: Alexei
characters.description: Alexei19851984Biographical informationStatusDeceasedBorn1950sDiedJuly 4,1985AgeEarly 30s[1]AliasesRussianScott Clarke(byJim HoppertoJoyce Byers)Smirnoff(byHopper)Soviet scum(byMurray Bauman)Traitor(byGrigori)ResidenceRussia(195?-1985)Hawkins,Indiana(1985)Other relationsMurray Bauman(friend)Joyce Byers(friend)Jim Hopper(former enemy; acquaintance/avenger)Grigori†(former ally; enemy/killer)Russian Scientist†(former co-worker)Ozerov(former ally; enemy)Dr. Zharkov†(former ally; enemy)Stepanov(former employer; enemy)AffiliationSoviet Union(betrayed)OccupationScientistPhysical informationHeight6’0” (182cm)Eye colorBrownHair colorBrownPortrayalMain actorAlec UtgoffAppears in18,22,23,24 Smirnoff(byHopper)Soviet scum(byMurray Bauman)Traitor(byGrigori) Dr. Alexei, portrayed byAlec Utgoff, was a recurring character in thethird seasonofStranger Things. On the surface, Alexei appeared to be a standard, albeit very capable, scientist. In reality, he had a very jovial and almost child-like personality. During his brief partnership with Hopper, Joyce and Murray, Alexei was exposed to relative luxuries that were likely unavailable to him in the USSR. He was excited by the sight of a 7-Eleven and eagerly tried the confectionaries, resulting in a particular love for cherry-flavoured Slurpees. Back at Murray's home, Alexei was able to watch cartoons and enjoyed Looney Tunes and Woody Woodpecker and laughed along with glee. He also longed to join the festivities at the Hawkins Fun Fair. When Murray decided to take him there, Alexei was overjoyed, especially when he won a game of Balloon Darts and was given a giant Woody Woodpecker plush. Alexei enjoyed the American way of life so much that he was willing to defect from the USSR and asked Murray about the possibility of becoming a US citizen once they had stopped his Russian comrades. Alexei demonstrated a keen fear of authority figures, whether they were his Russian superiors or Jim Hopper holding him hostage. On one occasion, he attempted to be bolder when Jim Hopper gave him a strawberry Slurpee instead of his favourite cherry flavour. Alexei cockily refused to divulge information on the Russian's plan unti he got the correct flavour. This cocky facade quickly dropped, however, when Hopper became infuriated and attacked him, causing Alexei to scream in terror. Alexei was then given the opportunity to take a car and drive back to his superiors, but stopped after deducing he would likely be tortured and killed for cooperating with Americans. He also shows great accuracy and precision, as he won a game of Balloon Darts without missing a single balloon.

characters.name: Andy Harper
characters.description: Andy Harper19871986Biographical informationStatusAliveBornBetweenAugust 1969andMarch 1970[1]Age16[2](season four)17-18(season five)ResidenceHawkins,IndianaRelationship statusSingleOther relationsJason Carver†(friend/teammate)Chance(friend/teammate)Patrick McKinney†(friend/teammate)Hunter(friend/teammate)Paul(friend/teammate)Lucas Sinclair(former friend; teammate/enemy)Eddie Munson†(enemy)Erica Sinclair(enemy)Dustin Henderson(enemy)Mike Wheeler(enemy)Will Byers(enemy)Steve Harrington(indirect enemy)AffiliationHawkins High SchoolOccupationStudent atHawkins High School(1984-1988)Basketball player on theHawkins High Basketball Team(formerly)Physical informationHeight5’10” (178cm)Eye colorBlueHair colorBrownPortrayalMain actorClayton Royal JohnsonAppears in26|27|28|29|30|31|33|3435|42Andy Harper,[3]portrayed byClayton Royal Johnson, is a recurring antagonist in thefourthandfifthseasons ofStranger Things. He isJason's friend and teammate. Andy was born around 1969-1970. Nothing is known about his family life. In 1984, Andy attendedHawkins High Schooland was friends withJason Carver,Patrick McKinneyandChance. They became close and joined the basketball team together. He initially appeared at the basketball match, where he, Jason, and other teammates celebrated their victory by spending the night at Benny's Burgers. The next morning, Andy is watching the most recent news while learning about the recent murder of a student from Hawkins High. When Andy makes fun of the student's death without realizing it is Jason's girlfriend, he offends everyone. Andy is shown to be crueler and more ruthless than the rest of his basketball teammates. He has no qualms about making insensitive jokes, like mocking the death of a student before realizing that the dead student was Jason's girlfriend,Chrissy Cunningham. Andy is also very self-centered as he mocked those who were not as popular as him and his friends. He was even confrontational towards Lucas over his familiarity withHellfireand even after discovering his association with Hellfire, Andy was quick to turn against him. After the death of his fellow teammate, Patrick, Andy grew more aggressive. In addition, Andy was willing to hurt younger children where he threatened to breakErica Sinclair's arms when he pinned her down, despite her being a minor. In 1987, Andy's behavior is even worse as he physically attacksDustinseveral times because of his loyalty to Eddie. He even goes so far as to kill his snake and vandalize Eddie's grave with its blood out of petty revenge before violently beating Dustin with his teammates. However, in 1989, Andy seemed to not hold it against Dustin when Dustin honored Eddie by wearing a Hellfire Club T-shirt at Hawkins High School's graduation ceremony. This could mean he has become more humble as time has gone by, or he just knew it was far too public to start a fight with him.

characters.name: Angela
characters.description: AngelaBiographical informationStatusAliveBorn1971Age14-15(season four)ResidenceLenora Hills,CaliforniaRelationship statusDatingJake[1]Other relationsJake(boyfriend)[1]Stacy(best friend)Chad(best friend)Eleven(enemy)OccupationStudent atLenora Hills High SchoolPhysical informationHeight5'5" (165 cm)Eye colorHazelHair colorBlondePortrayalMain actorElodie Grace OrkinAppears in26,27,28Angela, portrayed byElodie Grace Orkin, is a recurring antagonist in thefourth seasonofStranger Things. Angela was the 'queen bee' ofLenora Hills High School. She would frequently bully fellow studentJane "El" Hopper, ganging up on her with the help of her boyfriendJakeand her close friendsStacyandChad. When Angela got in trouble for destroying El's class project, Angela and her friends irrationally decided to get payback by humiliating El at the localroller rink. Unfortunately, following the incident, El finally lost her temper with Angela and struck her in the face with a skate, giving Angela a concussion where she was left overwhelmed and in pain. Angela was a popular and confident girl with a very cruel and vindictive personality. She had no qualms bullyingEl, taking advantage of her timid nature and ruthlessly humiliating her while never bothering to leave her alone. Angela seemed to possess no morals between right and wrong where she was quite shocked when she got sent to the principal's office, claiming she did not do anything wrong while ignoring the fact that she encouraged her boyfriend and friends to destroy El's project. While she knew better than to throwJakeor her friends under the bus, Angela cowardly begged El to back up, believing El would help her, despite having bullied her. Angela was even shown to be very illogical, calling El a "snitch" despite the latter never having told on her and missing the point that it was Angela herself who got caught in the act. Angela also held no remorse for her cruel actions and did not hesitate to violate boundaries; she mocked El's presumed deceasedfather, which led to El retaliating, striking her in the face with a roller skate.

characters.description: AngelaBiographical informationStatusAliveBorn1971Age14-15(season four)ResidenceLenora Hills,CaliforniaRelationship statusDatingJake[1]Other relationsJake(boyfriend)[1]Stacy(best friend)Chad(best friend)Eleven(enemy)OccupationStudent atLenora Hills High SchoolPhysical informationHeight5'5" (165 cm)Eye colorHazelHair colorBlondePortrayalMain actorElodie Grace OrkinAppears in26,27,28Angela, portrayed byElodie Grace Orkin, is a recurring antagonist in thefourth seasonofStranger Things. Angela was the 'queen bee' ofLenora Hills High School. She would frequently bully fellow studentJane "El" Hopper, ganging up on her with the help of her boyfriendJakeand her close friendsStacyandChad. When Angela got in trouble for destroying El's class project, Angela and her friends irrationally decided to get payback by humiliating El at the localroller rink. Unfortunately, following the incident, El finally lost her temper with Angela and struck her in the face with a skate, giving Angela a concussion where she was left overwhelmed and in pain. Angela was a popular and confident girl with a very cruel and vindictive personality. She had no qualms bullyingEl, taking advantage of her timid nature and ruthlessly humiliating her while never bothering to leave her alone. Angela seemed to possess no morals between right and wrong where she was quite shocked when she got sent to the principal's office, claiming she did not do anything wrong while ignoring the fact that she encouraged her boyfriend and friends to destroy El's project. While she knew better than to throwJakeor her friends under the bus, Angela cowardly begged El to back up, believing El would help her, despite having bullied her. Angela was even shown to be very illogical, calling El a "snitch" despite the latter never having told on her and missing the point that it was Angela herself who got caught in the act. Angela also held no remorse for her cruel actions and did not hesitate to violate boundaries; she mocked El's presumed deceasedfather, which led to El retaliating, striking her in the face with a roller skate.

characters.name: Dmitri Antonov
characters.description: Dmitri AntonovBiographical informationStatusAliveAgeApproximately 40AliasesEnzoCommie Bastard(byHopper)Dull Man(byYuri)ResidenceRussiaRelationship statusMarriedFamilyUnnamed wifeMikhail Antonov(son)Other relationsYuri Ismaylov(former enemy; friend)Jim Hopper(friend)Joyce Byers(friend)Murray Bauman(friend)Ivan†(former friend; enemy)Warden Melnikov†(former boss; enemy)Oleg†(fellow prisoner; friend)AffiliationKamchatka Facility,Soviet Ministry of Internal Affairs(formerly)OccupationSergeant majorPrison guardPrisoner(all formerly)Physical informationHeight6'0" (182 cm)Weight154-170 lbs.Eye colorBlueHair colorDark BlondePortrayalMain actorTom WlaschihaAppears in27,28,29,30,31,32,33,34Dmitri "Enzo" Antonov (Дмитрий "Энцо" Антонов), is a recurring character in thefourth seasonofStranger Things. He is portrayed byTom Wlaschiha.[1] Dmitri was aSovietprison guard stationed at amilitary facilityinKamchatka, and a member of the Soviet Ministry of Internal Affairs. He struck up a complicated friendship with theAmericanprisonerJim Hopperand helped Hopper find a means of escape; however, after being double-crossed by akey contact, Antonov was stripped of his rank, and imprisoned for his treasonous activities. Antonov and Hopper then banded together to survive the wrath ofthe Soviets' captured Demogorgon. At some point, Dmitri married and had a son named Mikhail. His uniform indicated that he was a member of the Soviet Ministry of Internal Affairs, and held the rank of "starshina", a senior enlisted man, roughly the equivalent of a sergeant major in the U.S. Army. Dmitri is described as "smart and charming" and has a dry sense of humor. As seen by his willingness to assist Hopper in escaping Kamchatka, he seems to have no special allegiance to his government or duties. However, Dmitri does seem to be loyal to his country's people and his family as evident when he confronted Yuri Ismaylov by explaining how the monsters from the Upside Down would kill their loved ones if they did not stop them from conquering the world. He is also determined to get back to his son through any means necessary. In addition, Dmitri does have a positive streak and is willing to take opportunities and risks, showing he is a brave man. He also has a sense of honor as he was impressed with Hopper's determination and kept his promises with him while calling Yuri out for losing his sense of dignity.

characters.name: Argyle
characters.description: ArgyleBiographical informationStatusAliveBorn1967-1968Age17-18 (season four)ResidenceLenora Hills,CaliforniaFamilyAmado[1](cousin)Other relationsJonathan Byers(best friend)Eleven(friend)Will Byers(friend)Mike Wheeler(friend)Eden Bingham(friend/love interest)AffiliationSurfer Boy PizzaOccupationStudent atLenora Hills High SchoolEmployee atSurfer Boy PizzaPhysical informationHeight6'1" (185 cm)Eye colorBrownHair colorDark brownPortrayalMain actorEduardo FrancoAppears in26,27,28,29,30,31,33,34 26,27,28,29,30,31,33,34 March 24,1986 In comparison to his quiet and shy best friend Jonathan, Argyle is extremely laid-back and relaxed, most likely stemming from his 4/20 lifestyle and smoking marijuana. Argyle is shown to panic rather easily at gruesome and gory sights, asking panicked questions as Jonathan, Mike, Will, and Agent Harmon ordered him to drive. When burying Harmon's corpse, Argyle began to have a panic attack over the revelation, and it wasn't until Jonathan suggested that he smoked more weed that he eventually calmed down. When seeing a plethora of corpse surrounding Eleven, Argyle once again began to hyperventilate. Argyle is also shown to be a very kind person. He constantly has an uplifting attitude and has no problems with driving his friends places inhis van. Additionally, when Agent Harmon died from his wounds, Argyle crafted a makeshift headstone for him. Despite not even knowing Harmon's name, Argyle wanted to commemorate his sacrifice. However, this also showed that Argyle seems to lack critical thinking, as he tried to sign the headstone with all of their names, despite them trying to leave no trace of their whereabouts. This was seen earlier when Argyle arrived at theByers' residenceand saw several military-style SUVs parked on the lawn. He apparently didn't consider this strange and instead assumed they belonged to guests of a party Jonathan was throwing. Despite this, Argyle is not outright unintelligent. When he, Jonathan, Mike and Will were lost trying to find The Nina Project, Argyle stumbled upon some tires in the dirt and correctly identified them as military, finally pointing them all in the right direction.

The gang would pursue and kill the peopleㅤfrom their pasts who had wronged them. On October 29, 1984, the gang had traveled toPittsburgh, Pennsylvania. The gang then fled to Chicago, residing in an abandoned warehouse.

Although an investigative journalist, Murray was always ignored by others due to his eccentric behavior and strange reports in solving investigations. After being hired by the Holland family to investigate their daughter's disappearance, he started working as an investigator, following over 200 tips to try to discoverBarbara Holland's whereabouts. While he was ignored by most people, he was sure that theHawkins National Laboratorywas somehow responsible for the abnormal events occurring in Hawkins and the death of Barbara and was committed to uncovering the truth. He had a good ability to analyze a person's character and personality. He recognized Jonathan and Nancy's feelings for each other and later made a similar deduction about Hopper and Joyce. His accurate psychoanalysis could make those he analyzed very uncomfortable. Murray is also shown to be incredibly nonchalant and relaxed, as he casually waves and sips a drink at the people who despise him. He was also shown to be very clever when he explained that the tape evidence provided by Nancy and Jonathan could easily be discredited, but a modified tape which was more believable to the public could expose the lab. Despite his rough demeanor, Murray had a soft side to him, overcoming his hatred of Russians in bonding with the scientist Alexei, teaching him about American culture through cartoons, food, and carnival games. When Alexei was murdered before his eyes, he was left devastated, trying to grapple with the fact he had left him unprotected just to get a corn dog. Murray was shown to have a high esteem because of his black-belt in karate. characters.description: Fred BensonBiographical informationStatusDeceasedBornBetween March1969and March1970DiedMarch 22,1986Age16[1]AliasesFreddieResidence63 Cherry Street[2]Hawkins,IndianaOther relationsNancy Wheeler(friend/crush/classmate)Ms. Kelley(counselor)Vecna†(enemy/killer)AffiliationHawkins High School(formerly)OccupationStudent atHawkins High School(1983/1984-1986)Journalist forThe Weekly Streak(19??-1986)Physical informationHeight5’7” (170cm)Eye colorBlueHair colorBrownPortrayalMain actorLogan Riley BrunerAppears in26,27,28(corpse)Fred Benson, portrayed byLogan Riley Bruner, was a recurring character in thefourth seasonofStranger Things. Fred was a student ofHawkins High Schooland a contributor toThe Weekly Streak, the school newspaper. In1986, Fred was fatally"cursed"byVecna, with Fred's trauma from the previous year coming back to haunt him. At some point in1985, Fred was in a car accident that caused the death ofanother student. He sustained facial injuries himself in this accident, which caused a scar across his left cheek. He was wracked with guilt ever since. Fred seemed to be just a stereotypical '80s nerd who is clever and yet kind-hearted. However, he showed quite a bit of wit and snark towards Nancy, typically regardingJonathan Byers. He was intelligent and passionate about journalism, to the point that Nancy invited him along to investigate the murder of Chrissy, with her. Unlike the reporters atThe Hawkins Post, Fred treated Nancy with respect. Though Fred appeared to be bright and level-headed, he harbored extreme guilt for his role in the fatal 1985 car accident, and believed many people look at him as a "murderer". This guilt and mental unrest made him a target of Vecna.

characters.name: Billy's mother
characters.description: Billy's motherBiographical informationStatusUnknownAgeLate 20s[1](1970sflashback)Relationship statusDivorced fromNeil HargroveFamilyBilly Hargrove†(son)Neil Hargrove(ex-husband)Other relationsWendy(friend)Physical informationEye colorBlueHair colorBlondePortrayalMain actorBeth RiesgrafAppears in23,25,34An unnamed woman, portrayed byBeth Riesgraf, is a minor character in thethird seasonofStranger Thingsand was themotherofBilly Hargrove. Billy's mother lived in California with her son, Billy, and her husbandNeil Hargrove. She had a friend named Wendy. She loved Billy and supported his passion for surfing. Still, Neil's accusations of her cheating on him and physical abuse towards her eventually drove her to abandon her son to escape. Billy tried to get his mother to come home, but to no avail. His mother's departure allowed Neil to gain full custody of Billy and abuse him even further, turning him into a cruel, violent bully. By 1985, the Hargroves and the Mayfields had moved toHawkins,Indiana. There, Billy waspossessedbyan entityfromanother dimension. To break him out of this possession, a girl namedElevenreminded Billy of his mother, providing a description of her and a day when Billy and his mother were at the beach. Soon afterwards, Billy was killed bythe proxy bodyof the entity. Billy's mother was much kinder and loving to Billy than Neil was. She seemed to enjoy spending time with her son on the beach away from Neil. When she had enough of Neil's abuse, she divorced him, leaving Billy to be raised by his father. She was not a pushover, as she struck Neil over the head when he cornered her spewing presumably false accusations.

characters.name: Eden Bingham
characters.description: Eden BinghamBiographical informationStatusAliveBorn1967-1968Age17-18ResidenceBingham House,Salt Lake City,UtahFamilyMr. Bingham(Father)Mrs. Bingham(Mother)Suzie Bingham(Sister)Tabitha Bingham(Sister)Cornelius Bingham(Brother)Sterling Bingham(Brother)Tanner Bingham(Brother)Tatum Bingham(Sister)Peter Bingham(Brother)Unnamed brothers and sistersOther relationsArgyle(Friend/Love Interest)AffiliationThe Bingham FamilyPhysical informationEye colorBrown/HazelHair colorBlackPortrayalMain actorAudrey HolcombAppears in31Eden Bingham, portrayed byAudrey Holcomb, is a minor character who appears in thefourth seasonofStranger Things. She is presumably the eldest ofSuzie Bingham's many siblings. Mike Wheeler,Will Byers,Jonathan Byers, andArgylearrived at theBingham Housein need to locateNINA. After walking through and discovering the chaos inside, the quartet bumped into Eden putting her youngest sibling, Cornelius, in timeout for turning off the power. She saw Argyle and asked him who he was, leaving Argyle to introduce himself, whilst stuttering. After the group asked where Suzie was, Eden directed them to the third floor. She told them to give "that four-eyed little shit" a shove for her, causing Argyle to say he would "do anything" for her. They ceased to break eye contact as Eden slowly walked back down the stairs. While Suzie, Mike, Will, and Jonathan snuck into Suzie's father's office, Argyle slipped away. Appearing to be the eldest Bingham sibling, Eden is shown to be in charge of the household while her father works in his office. This in turn leaves her incredibly moody due to her siblings having no control or filter whatsoever. Unlike her family, Eden doesn't appear to be religious, wearing goth clothes and openly swearing in front of Suzie, teasingly asking her if she'll, "go to hell". When meeting Argyle, Eden is instantly smitten for him, which leads them to smoke weed in hisSurfer Boy Pizzavan, only to be discovered by Suzie and Argyle's friends.

characters.name: Mr. Bingham
characters.description: Mr. BinghamBiographical informationStatusAliveResidenceBingham House,Salt Lake City,UtahRelationship statusMarriedFamilyUnnamed wifeEden Bingham(daughter)Tabitha Bingham(daughter)Suzie Bingham(daughter)Tatum Bingham(daughter)Cornelius Bingham(son)Sterling Bingham(son)Tanner Bingham(son)Peter Bingham(son)OccupationClergyman/Religious Official(presumed)Physical informationEye colorBrownHair colorBrownPortrayalMain actorMark RoweAppears in31 Mr. Bingham, portrayed byMark Rowe, is a minor character appearing in thefourth seasonofStranger Things. He is the patriarch of theBingham Family, a large Latter-day Saint (Mormon) household residing inSalt Lake City, Utah. A devout and strict father, Mr. Bingham maintains a rigid domestic environment, though he often remains secluded in his locked home office, leaving his eldest daughter,Eden, to manage the chaotic behavior of his eight children. Mr. Bingham is characterized as a stern, traditionalist patriarch. He is easily overwhelmed by the "hooliganism" of his younger sons but remains firm in his religious convictions. His intolerance for intellectual dissent within his household is highlighted by his seizure of Suzie's technology, which he viewed as a gateway to beliefs that challenged the family’s faith.

characters.name: Suzie Bingham
characters.description: Suzie Bingham19861985Biographical informationStatusAliveBorn1971Age13-14(season three)14-15(season four)AliasesSuzie-Poo(byDustin)Four-Eyed Shit(byEden)ResidenceBingham House,Salt Lake City,UtahRelationship statusUnknown[1]FamilyUnnamed motherMr. Bingham(father)Eden Bingham(sister)Tabitha Bingham(sister)Cornelius Bingham(brother)Sterling Bingham(brother)Tanner Bingham(brother)Tatum Bingham(sister)Peter Bingham(brother)Unnamed brothers and sistersOther relationsDustin Henderson(boyfriend or ex-boyfriend)Mike Wheeler(friend)Will Byers(friend)Jonathan Byers(friend)Argyle(friend)AffiliationCamp Know WhereOccupationHigh School StudentPhysical informationHeight5’3” (160cm)Eye colorBrownHair colorDark BrownPortrayalMain actorGabriella PizzoloAppears in2526|31 Suzie Bingham, portrayed byGabriella Pizzolo, is a recurring character inStranger Things, first appearing as a minor character in thethird seasonbefore returning as a recurring character in thefourth season. A native ofSalt Lake City, Utah, she became the girlfriend ofDustin Hendersonin1985after the two met atCamp Know Where. Suzie was born to her unnamed parents in Salt Lake City, Utah. She was raised in a strict Mormon household, but while also devoted to her faith, she also developed more nerdy interests, such as science, technology, and reading fantasy novels. Like Dustin, Suzie has a strong fascination with science and comics. Due to her religious family and appearance, it's likely Suzie is sheltered and quiet, which leads to her nerdy nature. When Dustin calls Suzie asking if she knows the Planck constant, she jokingly remarks, "Do you know the Earth orbits the sun?", reciting the complicated number by heart, showing her to be an intelligent and studious girl. It is shown that she can operate a ham radio, as that is her main mode of communication with Dustin. Her primary interests, based on objects in her room and mentions from Dustin, seem to be computer science, math, geography, ecology, and astronomy. She is also quite the romantic, being very joyous to hear from Dustin and seems to particularly enjoy singing with him. It is also shown in the comics that she was the one who first developed an interest in Dustin, and also confessed and asked him to be her boyfriend first, implying a certain amount of boldness. There's also some hints of jealousy to Suzie, as when she hears Erica murmur to Dustin to get the number already, Suzie suspiciously asks who that was, but Dustin lies by saying it was someone overlapping the frequency. Her sensitive personality is shown when Suzie gets upset when Dustin refuses to sing, enough to almost hang up on him, as well as running away in tears when Dustin mistakes her for someone else and yelling at her in the forest. She seems to be rather guilt-prone, a possible consequence of how she was raised, becoming so upset at helping Dustin change his grade that she cannot sleep, and her father begins to notice her distress. She also cries and pleads with her father

characters.name: Captain Brenner
characters.description: Captain BrennerBiographical informationStatusDeceasedFamilyDr. Martin Brenner†(son)OccupationUS Navy captainCaptain Brennerwas a character fromStranger Things: The First Shadow. Brenner, the captain of theUSSEldridge, was the sole survivor of a disastrous experiment that transported his ship toDimension X. Brenner returned to the human world with significant injuries and a unique blood type.[1][2] By 1943, Captain Brenner had become the commanding officer of theUSS Eldridge, a US Navy vessel involved in classified government research collectively referred to as"Project Rainbow"and thePhiladelphia Experiment.

Despite how manipulating and deceiving Brenner is consistently shown to be, he appears to truly care for Eleven, seen when he risked his life carrying her to safety

characters.personality_section: As a researcher and director ofHawkins National Laboratory, Dr. Brenner was highly manipulative, calculative, ruthless and ambitious. He was solely responsible for the abduction ofTerry Ives's daughter,Eleven, to experiment on the child's dormant psychokinetic abilities. His atrocious nature was evident when he forced Eleven's mother to undergo electroshock therapy after she attempted to retrieve her daughter, frying Terry's brain and permanently damaging her mental state. He was shown to be vicious when Eleven disobeyed him as he used to put her through cruel psychological torture and forcefully kept her in solitary confinement, even though she was just a child. He would also encourage her to push the limits of her psychic abilities, caring little about the toil she had to suffer. However, if she performed well, Brenner would "reward" her with affection and endearment. Brenner would also adopt extreme measures to cover up the events ofthe Upside Down, for instance, manufacturing thefake body of Willeven though Will was actually kidnapped, sending military agents and helicopters to take Eleven back as well as imprisoning Joyce and Hopper to compel them to reveal her location in exchange for saving Will. He has little concern for the lives of his men although he did appear genuinely distraught over Shepard’s death. Despite how manipulating and deceiving Brenner is consistently shown to be, he appears to truly care for Eleven, seen when he risked his life carrying her to safety

He is the oldest son ofJoyce Byers, the older brother ofWill Byers, Ex-boyfriend ofNancy Wheeler, and the best friend ofArgyle. Jonathan is smart, introverted, asocial, kind, independent, and brave. Despite being a small-town boy, according toJoyce, he wants to go to college at New York University. He has a strong bond with his family and is implied to have a soft spot for animals, as he cried for a week after his tenth birthday when his father forced him to kill a rabbit on a hunting trip. He also has a keen interest in music, creatingseveral mixtapesfor himself and his brother, and is an aspiring photographer. In1984, Jonathan's personality managed to change a little bit. He became protective of Will, but not as much as his mother. He even encouraged Will to not let his differences bother him and that it's okay to be different not matter what. Jonathan tries to be by his brother's side despite it being difficult as Jonathan noted that Will has changed a lot since his time in the Upside Down. Jonathan was also not too happy with his mother's relationship withBob Newbyas both he and Joyce suffered from constant verbal abuse from his father than Will ever did. Jonathan also became friends withSteve Harringtonand was on good terms with him. Jonathan also developed feelings forNancy Wheeler, who shared his trauma, but struggled to accept them until he was encouraged byM

May 1989-)Relationship statusEngagedFamilyLonnie Byers(ex-husband)Jim Hopper(fiancé)Jonathan Byers(son)Will Byers(son)Eleven(former ward/daughter figure)Darlene(aunt)Other relationsBob Newby†(late boyfriend)Karen Wheeler(friend)Scott Clarke(friend)Sam Owens(friend)Murray Bauman(friend)Alexei†(friend)Martin Brenner†(enemy)Grigori†(enemy)Warden Melnikov†(enemy)Yuri Ismaylov(former enemy; ally)Dmitri Antonov(ally)Vecna†(enemy; victim)Chester†(pet)AffiliationByers familyMelvald's General Store(formerly)Wheeler familyOccupationRetail clerk atMelvald's General Store(1973-1985)Phone saleswoman(1985/1986-)Physical informationHeight5'3" (161 cm)Eye colorBrownHair colorBrownPortrayalMain actorWinona RyderOther actorsBirdy(teen)Isabella Pappas(The First Shadow)Appears in1|2|3|4|5|6|7|89|10|11|12|13|14|15|16|1718|19|20|21|22|23|24|2526|27|28|29|30|31|32|33|3435|36|37|38|39|40|41|42 November 6,1987 Joyce is highly generous, amicable, and a tremendously benevolent mother who would do almost anything to keep her sons, particularly Will, out of harm's way. After Will's disappearance, Joyce enlisted Chief Jim Hopper to find him and posted several missing-person posters with the help of her other son, Jonathan. Even after Will's presumed dead body was found, she was still convinced that her son was alive and undertook extreme measures to contact with him, such as chopping a hole in the wall of her house to find him. Ultimately, her commitment was rewarded when it was revealed that Will was alive, and, with Hopper's help, she saved him. However, when it was found that Will had been experiencing visions of the Upside Down, Joyce wasted no time bringing him to Dr. Owens for routine medical exams. Her extreme concern for Will led her to discover that the shadow creature from the Upside Down had overwhelmed and possessed him, causing him to behave strangely. After Will convulsed and suffered from severe pain during the burning of the tendrils, Joyce was terribly dismayed to learn that her son started showing memory problems, which caused her to berate the doctors. Eventually, it was her immense determination to protect her son that allowed her to save Will after she forced the shadow monster out by exposing her possessed son to extreme heat. By the summer of 1985, Joyce had shed her overprotectiveness of Will and accepted Jonathan's relationship withNancy Wheeler
